behavior_summary: U8P_WATRSPEL::func0080 implements the runtime for a ranged "water spell" or thrown-item attack: it waits for a target selection, computes direction-dependent x/y offsets, launches a projectile with Item::hurl, and then notifies the target of a hit via METHOD::receiveHitWrapper.
The function treats a suspended selection result of 0 as cancellation, spawning a cleanup helper (spawn ->FREE::func29D8(0)) and re-suspending when cancelled.
Direction from caster to target is resolved with Item::getDirToItem which returns a direction code (0..7) used to set horizontal (local2) and vertical (local3) throw parameters.

After calling Item::hurl(0x02, 0x0C, local3, local2, &local1) the process suspends (waiting for the hurl lifecycle) and then calls METHOD::receiveHitWrapper(0x01, local4, 0x01, &local1) to apply or announce the hit to the captured target.
notable_control_flow: SuspendAssign captures the player's selection into local1 and the code immediately checks local1==0 to detect cancellation.
On cancellation the function spawns FREE::func29D8(0) (likely cleanup) then suspends/returns control, preventing the hurl from proceeding.
Normal flow: query process target(), call Item::getDirToItem to get direction (local4), set local2/local3 from a per-direction table, call Item::hurl, suspend, then call METHOD::receiveHitWrapper.
Direction mapping is branch-like (IfStatements) selecting numeric offsets for eight cardinal/semi-cardinal directions (0..7).
open_questions: What exactly does spawn ->FREE::func29D8(0) do on cancellation (cleanup, UI reset, resource free, or something else)?
What are the semantic meanings of the numeric constants passed to Item::hurl (0x02, 0x0C) and receiveHitWrapper (0x01, 0x01) beyond inferred category/small-flag roles?
Does Item::hurl take the last parameter strictly as a pointer-to-target or as an in/out item handle (the code passes &local1)?
Are there additional side-effects (animation state, sound, global flags) triggered elsewhere when this process runs that are not visible in this excerpt?

main_function.name: U8P_WATRSPEL::func0080
main_function.purpose: Run the cast/throw sequence for a water-attack projectile: await a selected target, convert direction to trajectory offsets, spawn the projectile, and deliver the hit to the target.
entry_conditions: Invoked when a throw/cast action is initiated (inferred).
Function suspends waiting for a selection/target; resumes when SuspendAssign assigns local1.

function_map.name: U8P_WATRSPEL::func0080
function_map.role: cast/throw runtime for a ranged water spell or thrown item; handles target selection, cancellation, hurl spawning, and hit notification

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: resolve direction code (0..7) from caster to target; provides data for trajectory offset selection

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: spawn/launch a projectile with parameters (type, speed/height/flags, y-offset, x-offset, pointer-to-target)

function_map.name: METHOD::receiveHitWrapper
function_map.role: dispatch/apply hit to target after projectile lifecycle completes

function_map.name: process target()
function_map.role: query the current process id for targeting context

function_map.name: FREE::func29D8
function_map.role: spawned helper/cleanup routine executed on selection cancel (observed as spawn ->FREE::func29D8(0))

external_calls.purpose: Query current target/process id before selection (inferred preparatory check).
Spawn a cleanup/reset helper when the captured selection is 0 (cancellation).
Compute direction code from caster to target (0..7) used to pick x/y offsets for the projectile.
Launch the projectile with parameters (inferred call signature: type=0x02, height/speed/flag=0x0C, y-offset=local3, x-offset=local2, targetPtr=&local1).
Notify/apply hit to the selected target after the hurl lifecycle (args include damage/type flags and direction code).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_WATRSPEL::func0080 implements the runtime for a ranged "water spell" or thrown-item attack: it waits for a target selection, computes direction-dependent x/y offsets, launches a projectile with Item::hurl, and then notifies the target of a hit via METHOD::receiveHitWrapper.
